title: Cyathocalyx olivaceus
taxonomy: Cyathocalyx olivaceus is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is part of the larger order of Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Annonaceae includes over 2,200 species, and Cyathocalyx olivaceus is one of them. The classification of Cyathocalyx olivaceus is further divided into kingdom Plantae, and phylum Angiospermophyta. The specific classification of Cyathocalyx olivaceus is: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Magnoliales, family Annonaceae, genus Cyathocalyx, and species C. olivaceus.
uses: Cyathocalyx olivaceus is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia. The roots and other parts of the plant are used to treat various health conditions. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. The plant is also used to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cyathocalyx olivaceus for medicinal purposes.
